name: sparrow-wifi
title: sparrow-wifi
description:
Graphical Wi-Fi Analyzer for Linux
This package contains a graphical Wi-Fi analyzer for Linux. It provides a more
comprehensive GUI-based replacement for tools like inSSIDer and linssid that
runs specifically on Linux. In its most comprehensive use cases, sparrow-wifi
integrates Wi-Fi, software-defined radio (hackrf), advanced bluetooth tools
(traditional and Ubertooth), traditional GPS (via gpsd), and drone/rover GPS
via mavlink in one solution.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for sparrow-wifi.
arguments.raw: -wifi  Option present in package usage line for sparrow-wifi.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sparrow-wifi because Graphical Wi-Fi Analyzer for Linux.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sparrow-wifi as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sparrow-wifi [options] [args]` as documented upstream for sparrow-wifi.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sparrow-wifi with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sparrow-wifi --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -wifi
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for sparrow-wifi.
